name: Ichirō Fujiyama
description: {{nihongo|'''Ichirō Fujiyama'''|藤山 一郎|Fujiyama Ichirō|April 8, 1911 – August 21, 1993}}, born {{nihongo|'''Takeo Masunaga'''|増永 丈夫|Masunaga Takeo}}, was a Japanese singer and composer, known for his contribution to Japanese popular music called ''[[ryūkōka]]'' by his Western classical music skills. He was born in [[Chūō, Tokyo]], and graduated from the [[Tokyo University of the Arts|Tokyo Music School]]. Although he was regarded as a [[tenor]] singer in Japanese popular music, he was originally a classical [[baritone]] singer. He also acted in various films, and was a close friend of [[Minoru Matsuya]] (1910–1995). His workroom has been reproduced inside the "[[NHK]] museum of broadcasting" as an exhibit.
